perturbation.original_text: 20. DISPUTE RESOLUTION 20.1 A Party that is of the view that a Dispute has arisen shall give written notice thereof to the other Parties, provided that such notice shall clearly identify the D ispute and provide full particularity thereof, and the Parties shall thereafter take immediate steps to attempt to resolve the Dispute through their senior executives or other representatives who have the necessary authority to make binding decisions with respect to such Dispute (Senior Executives).
perturbation.changed_text: 27. RELAXATION 27.1 A Party that is of the view that a Dispute has arisen shall give written notice thereof to the other Parties, provided that such notice shall clearly identify the D ispute and provide full particularity thereof, and the Parties shall thereafter take immediate steps to attempt to resolve the Dispute through their senior executives or other representatives who have the necessary authority to make binding decisions with respect to such Dispute (Senior Executives).
perturbation.explanation: Moving the dispute resolution clause into the 'Relaxation' section obscures its importance and makes it less likely to be noticed, potentially hindering the resolution of disputes. Placing the Dispute Resolution clause within the clause dealing with relaxation makes it harder to determine whether it is part of the main contract or an addendum.
perturbation.law_citation: Section 3 of the Arbitration Act 42 of 1965
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 3 of the Arbitration Act emphasizes the validity and enforceability of arbitration agreements. Relocating the dispute resolution clause to an unexpected section like 'Relaxation' could create ambiguity, potentially undermining its enforceability under the Act, because the relocation could suggest that the section is not an important aspect of the agreement.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 9 U.S. Code § 3 - Stay of proceedings where issue therein referable to arbitration | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 9 U.S. Code § 3 - Stay of proceedings where issue therein referable to arbitration U.S. Code Notes prev | next If any suit or proceeding be brought in any of the courts of the United States upon any issue referable to arbitration under an agreement in writing for such arbitration, the court in which such suit is pending, upon being satisfied that the issue involved in such suit or proceeding is referable to arbitration under such an agreement, shall on application of one of the parties stay the trial of the action until such arbitration has been had in accordance with the terms of the agreement, providing the applicant for the stay is not in default in proceeding with such arbitration. (July 30, 1947, ch. 392, 61 Stat. 670 .) Derivation Act Feb. 12, 1925, ch. 213, § 3, 43 Stat. 883 . U.S. Code Toolbox Law about... perturbation.justification: The change introduces a structural flaw by moving the dispute resolution clause into a section titled 'Relaxation.' This placement undermines the enforceability of the arbitration agreement because it obscures the clause's importance and makes it less likely to be noticed. The Law Explanation also supports this, indicating that such relocation could create ambiguity, potentially undermining its enforceability. Scraped Law Snippet 1, referencing 9 U.S. Code § 3, emphasizes the importance of arbitration agreements by stating that courts must stay proceedings if the issue is referable to arbitration under a written agreement. By burying the dispute resolution clause in an unexpected section, the modified contract creates ambiguity, making it harder to enforce the arbitration agreement, directly contradicting the intent of the law.

perturbation.original_text: 17. GOVERNING LAW 17.1 This Agreement shall be construed and interpreted i n accordance with the law in the Republic of South Africa. 17.2 The parties submit to the non -exclusive jurisdiction of the North Gauteng High Court, Pretoria or its successor and they agree that all disputes arising from the conclusion or interpretation of t his Agreement may be heard by such court.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. NO REPRESENTATION OR WARRANTY 9.1 Unless otherwise specifically agreed to by way of a written document signed by the Disclosing Party, the Disclosing Party – 9.1.1 does not give or make any warranty, representation or undertaking, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of any of the Confidential Information or other information re ceived by the Recipient or the Permitted Persons or as to the reasonableness of any assumptions on which any of the same is based; 9.1.2 shall not be responsible or in any way liable for the use of the Confidential Information by the Recipient or the Permitted P ersons; and; 9.1.3 is under no obligation to update or correct any inaccuracies which may become apparent in any of the Confidential Information. 17. GOVERNING LAW 17.1 This Agreement shall be construed and interpreted i n accordance with the law in the Republic of South Africa. 17.2 The parties submit to the non -exclusive jurisdiction of the North Gauteng High Court, Pretoria or its successor and they agree that all disputes arising from the conclusion or interpretation of t his Agreement may be heard by such court.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the governing law clause to the 'No Representation or Warranty' section is highly unusual. The governing law should be readily apparent, not buried under disclaimers. This relocation can mislead a party into thinking the governing law is less important or even inapplicable.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Principles of Contract Interpretation under South African Law
perturbation.law_citation: Common Law Principles of Contract Interpretation
perturbation.law_explanation: South African common law principles dictate that contracts should be interpreted to give effect to the intentions of the parties, which includes the clear identification of the governing law. Placing the governing law clause under 'No Representation or Warranty' obscures its intended significance, potentially undermining the principle that parties understand and agree to the legal framework governing their agreement.
perturbation.justification: The Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction definition states a contradiction exists when a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret. The Perturbation Explanation details that the Governing Law was moved under the 'No Representation or Warranty' section, and is considered a structural flaw. The Law Explanation states this obscures its intended significance undermining the principle that parties understand and agree to the legal framework governing their agreement. The contract change would likely fail legal scrutiny.

perturbation.original_text: 11. INDEMNITY The Recipient hereby accepts ful l liability for the maintenance of the confidentiality of the Confidential Information pertaining to the Disclosing Party and hereby (in addition to, and without affecting, any other rights or remedies the Disclosing Party may have) unconditionally and irr evocably indemnifies and holds the Disclosing Party and its Affiliates harmless against any and all actions, claims, demands, liabilities, damages, costs, losses or expenses (but specifically excluding any claims for indirect or consequential loss) directl y resulting from any breach by the Recipient, or any person to whom the Recipient has disclosed or given access to any part of the Confidential Information, of any of the provisions under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. PUBLICITY 15.1 Subject to in terms of clause 4, each Party undertakes to keep confidential and not to disclose to any third party, save as may be required in law (including by the rules and/or listings requirements of any securities exchange on which the securities of a Party or its Affiliates may be listed, where applicable) or permitted in terms of this Agreement, the nature, content or existence of this Agreement. 11. INDEMNITY The Recipient hereby accepts ful l liability for the maintenance of the confidentiality of the Confidential Information pertaining to the Disclosing Party and hereby (in addition to, and without affecting, any other rights or remedies the Disclosing Party may have) unconditionally and irr evocably indemnifies and holds the Disclosing Party and its Affiliates harmless against any and all actions, claims, demands, liabilities, damages, costs, losses or expenses (but specifically excluding any claims for indirect or consequential loss) directl y resulting from any breach by the Recipient, or any person to whom the Recipient has disclosed or given access to any part of the Confidential Information, of any of the provisions under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Relocating the indemnity clause under the 'Publicity' section is misleading. Indemnity deals with liability for breaches, not public announcements. This placement could lead a party to overlook the significant financial responsibilities outlined in the indemnity clause.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General Principles of Contract Law in South Africa
perturbation.law_explanation: South African contract law adheres to the principle of 'pacta sunt servanda,' meaning agreements must be honored. However, an indemnity clause outlines a crucial risk allocation and potential liability. Relocating the clause to the 'Publicity' section could impede the comprehension of this risk, making it less likely the parties understand the contract, weakening the effect of 'pacta sunt servanda'.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation involves a 'Structural Flaw Legal Contradiction,' specifically, relocating the indemnity clause to the 'Publicity' section. This action is misleading because indemnity clauses deal with liability for breaches, not public announcements. The law explanation states that this could impede the comprehension of risk allocation, weakening the effect of 'pacta sunt servanda.' The absence of scraped law snippets does not change the fact that placing a legally significant clause in a misleading section of a contract can obscure its importance and impact a party's understanding of their obligations, thus creating a legal contradiction. This falls under the definition of 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction' which says that 'a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret.'

perturbation.original_text: 19. DURATION The confidentiality undertaking as set out herein shall commence on the earliest of the first date on which any of the Parties released any Confidential Information to the other Party or the Ef fective Date and shall endure for a period of 10 (ten) years thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. PARTIES 1.1 The parties to this Agreement are: - TRANSNET ENGINEERING And Herewith referred to as the RECEIVING Parties 1.2 The parties agree as set out below. 19. DURATION The confidentiality undertaking as set out herein shall commence on the earliest of the first date on which any of the Parties released any Confidential Information to the other Party or the Ef fective Date and shall endure for a period of 10 (ten) years thereafter.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the duration clause to the 'Parties' section obscures a critical term of the agreement. The duration of confidentiality should be easily accessible, not buried in a section that primarily identifies the parties involved. This can lead to misunderstandings about how long confidentiality obligations last.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Contractual Certainty and 'Consensus ad Idem' in South African Law
perturbation.law_explanation: South African law requires contractual certainty, which means all essential terms must be clear and agreed upon. Placing the duration clause in an unexpected section like 'Parties' introduces uncertainty and potential ambiguity. The requirement for contractual certainty is reduced as a result.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that moving the duration clause to the 'Parties' section obscures a critical term of the agreement, potentially leading to misunderstandings about confidentiality obligations. The law explanation suggests that this violates the principle of contractual certainty under South African law. The structural flaws definition states that a legal contradiction arises when a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret. The changed text, by placing the duration clause within the 'Parties' section, buries a crucial term, thus making it difficult to locate and interpret, satisfying the definition of a structural flaw creating a legal contradiction.
